channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Timeline of the Menendez brothers’ murder case
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Prosecutors in Los Angeles say they will ask a court Friday to resentence Lyle and Erik Menendez, who were convicted of killing their parents in 1989 and are serving life without the possibility of parole.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Border Patrol makes massive meth haul in Texas
item.description: CBP said the seizure happened on October 18 at the Veterans International Bridge, which connects Brownsville, Texas, to Matamoros in Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: CDC updates COVID-19 vaccine recommendations for older adults
item.description: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention is urging some people to get additional doses of the COVID-19 vaccine for stronger protection against the virus.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KREX 5

item.title: McDonald's E. coli outbreak: Why only the Quarter Pounder was linked
item.description: McDonald's has voluntarily stopped selling its Quarter Pounder hamburger at restaurants across Colorado and other states after an E. coli outbreak was linked to the menu item.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, Fox 5

item.title: Kentucky lawmaker dies weeks after lawnmower plunges into swimming pool
item.description: Kentucky state Sen. Johnnie Turner (R) died Tuesday from injuries he suffered after being thrown into an empty swimming pool while riding his lawnmower just over a month ago.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Prosecutors seek resentencing for Erik and Lyle Menendez in 1989 killings of their parents
item.description: Prosecutors are recommending that Erik and Lyle Menendez be resentenced for the 1989 killings of their parents in Beverly Hills.
item.dc:creator: Stefanie Dazio, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: No end for Boeing labor strike as workers reject latest contract proposal
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Boeing factory workers have voted against the company’s latest contract offer and will remain on the picket lines six weeks into a strike that has stopped production of the aerospace giant’s bestselling jetliners.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Goldfish changes name of iconic crackers to appeal to adults
item.description: Snack lovers can look forward to a playful twist on a childhood favorite, as Goldfish announces a limited-time renaming of its iconic Cheddar crackers.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, ABC 4

item.title: Before Taylor Swift show in New Orleans, a homeless encampment is forced to move
item.description: In anticipation of three Taylor Swift concerts, which could draw around 150,000 visitors to New Orleans' Superdome this weekend, state authorities on Wednesday began clearing a nearby homeless encampment.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: People 50 and older should get pneumococcal vaccine, U.S. health officials recommend
item.description: U.S. health officials on Wednesday recommended that people 50 and older get a shot against bacteria that can cause pneumonia and other dangerous illnesses.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pennsylvania high court gives voters provisional option if their mail ballots get rejected
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Pennsylvania's highest court on Wednesday said people whose mail ballots are rejected for not following technical procedures in state law can cast provisional ballots, a decision sure to affect some of the thousands of mail-in votes likely to be rejected this fall.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A man who threatened to kill Democratic election officials pleads guilty
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A Colorado man repeatedly made online threats about killing the top elections officials in his state and Arizona — both Democrats — as well as a judge and law enforcement agents, according to a guilty plea he entered Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 50 years after Philadelphia halted prison medical testing, families seek reparations
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Fifty years ago, Philadelphia prison officials ended a medical testing program that had allowed an Ivy League researcher to conduct human testing on incarcerated people, many of them Black, for decades. Now, survivors of the program and their descendants want reparations.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bad state data may misdirect nearly $1 billion in federal funds to replace lead pipes
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — The Environmental Protection Agency is at risk of misallocating nearly $1 billion in lead pipe replacement funding to the wrong states because it didn’t verify inaccurate data provided by Texas and Florida, an agency watchdog announced.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tua Tagovailoa practices for the first time since suffering a concussion in Week 2
item.description:
Tyreek Hill couldn't hide his excitement catching the football from Tua Tagovailoa on Wednesday for the first time in over a month.
Tagovailoa practiced Wednesday for the first time since suffering a concussion in Week 2, and the Dolphins (2-4) are aiming for him to start Sunday against Arizona.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Federal appeals panel says suspended lawyer Michael Avenatti should be resentenced
item.description: PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — A federal appeals panel has vacated a 14-year sentence for suspended lawyer Michael Avenatti, who rose to fame representing porn actor Stormy Daniels against former President Donald Trump and was later convicted of bilking his California clients.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USPS urges voters not to delay mailing their ballots
item.description: Top officials at the U.S. Postal Service are urging voters to mail in their ballots at least one week before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, Fox 5

item.title: Video: Suspected drunken driver passes within feet of Harris' motorcade in Wisconsin
item.description: The driver was traveling in the wrong direction on the interstate in Milwaukee when deputies spotted him.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: After 36 years, accused killer in Oregon cold case to stand trial
item.description: An autopsy revealed Deborah Atrops was physically assaulted and strangled to death. She was 30. Then, the case went cold.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, Fox 5

item.title: 2 arrested after man claims kidnappers amputated his finger in Denver
item.description: Denver police arrested two people in connection to an alleged kidnapping and assault wherein the victim's finger was amputated by his captors.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KRON4

item.title: Surfer dies after being struck, impaled by swordfish
item.description: A longtime surfer and travel guide died after being impaled by a swordfish off the coast of Indonesia.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, Fox 5

item.title: Yelp turns off comments on the McDonald's that Trump worked at after fake reviews
item.description: Yelp recently paused reviews on a McDonald's in Pennsylvania after former President Trump served pre-screened customers at the location Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Want to be a Power Ranger? Original costumes, props up for auction
item.description: It's been over 30 years since the original Mighty Morphin Power Rangers television series aired in the United States, and for the kids who grew up watching the Power Rangers fight evil in Angel Grove, California, their chance to become one of them is now closer than ever.
item.dc:creator: Marco Rosas, Fox 5

item.title: MLB wants the Tampa Bay Rays to play in the area if hurricane-damaged Tropicana Field is unfit
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Major League Baseball hopes to find the Tampa Bay Rays a temporary home near their fanbase at least for the start of the 2025 season if the damage to Tropicana Field from Hurricane Milton cannot be repaired in time. The translucent fiberglass dome at the ballpark in St.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Severe E. coli outbreak linked to McDonald's quarter pounders
item.description: The CDC has issued a food safety alert for an outbreak of E. coli infections linked to McDonald's quarter pounders, with 49 people from ten states affected and one death reported.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Grieving families of seniors killed when a dock collapsed say the tragedy was preventable
item.description: JACKSONVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Regina Brinson heard a crack before the metal walkway gave way beneath her feet, plunging her into the water beneath the state-operated ferry dock on Georgia's Sapelo Island.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A Georgia teen accused in the Apalachee High School shooting has pleaded not guilty
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A 14-year-old Georgia boy indicted on murder charges in a mass shooting at Apalachee High School pleaded not guilty on Tuesday. Colt Gray's lawyer filed papers entering the plea after Gray was indicted on Thursday. They waived an arraignment hearing that had been scheduled for Nov.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: A Canadian Walmart store was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, ABC 4

item.title: Asheville annual holiday tradition canceled due to Helene
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (WSPA) - An annual national holiday tradition that has taken place for over three decades in Asheville has been canceled due to the aftermath of Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Nikolette Miller, Fox 5

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in Walmart oven
item.description: A Walmart store in eastern Canada was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven. Halifax Nova Scotia Regional Police said in a release that they were called at 9:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Jocelina Joiner, KTLA 5

item.title: Hurricane Oscar forms in the Caribbean
item.description: The formation of Oscar and Nadine comes as parts of the U.S. have been hit hard by a pair of hurricanes, Helene and Milton, that devastated communities, destroyed roads, caused power outages, took lives and injured many.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Meta again testing facial recognition software to fight 'celeb bait' scams
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Facebook and Instagram, is testing facial recognition software to combat “celeb bait” scams on its platforms as well as to allow users to more easily regain access to their accounts.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: PHOTOS: White House public tour gets makeover with 'reimagined' visitor experience
item.description: The White House public tour is getting an extreme makeover, with new digital exhibits, an expanded route and more access to rooms poised to be part of the "reimagined" experience for visitors.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WGN 9

item.title: Republicans head to court in expanding battle about overseas ballots
item.description: Overseas voting has become the latest battlefront in Republicans’ legal challenges leading up to the election.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Trump tells child there will be no cows under Harris
item.description: Former President Donald Trump joined Fox and Friends on Friday for an in-person interview where he told a 6-year-old child that the U.S. “won’t have any cows” if Vice President Kamala Harris is elected.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9
